figures.caption: Figure 11. Context size, agent processing throughput and pending agents with and without employing thrashing avoidance technique in KAIROS. This experiment runs mini-sweagent with DABStep with a request rate of 0.05 jobs/s and a target SLO of 20 tokens/s.

Figure 9. Change in instantaneous power, GPU frequency, and context size of KAIROS with respect to time for miniswe-agent running on DABStep with arrival rate of 0.03 jobs/s and SLO target of 35 tokens/s.
Figure 16. Launching id-tracker with Harbor and a context-aware router

Figure 7. Comparison of throughput, SLO attainment (20 tokens/s per-agent target), energy, and power across request rates for no frequency control, fixed 810 MHz, and KAIROS. KAIROS reduces power by 27% on average without sacrificing SLO.
Figure 15. Per-agent throughput distribution comparing a no frequency control baseline and KAIROS with different SLO targets of 20, 35, and 45 tokens/s.
